<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Type theory and HoTT</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Type theory and HoTT

A foundation that replaces 'membership in a set' with 'derivation of a typing judgment', then discovers that its identity types behave like spaces. From Π/Σ and the Curry–Howard correspondence to univalence, higher inductive types, and the simplicial-set model.

Set theory says 'mathematics is the study of what's in collections'. Type theory says 'mathematics is the study of what we can derive', and replaces the global $\in$ with a structured deduction system over typing judgments. Every term carries its type with it; well-formed expressions are the ones with a derivation. Layered on top of this — first by Martin-Löf, then by Voevodsky and the HoTT community — sits a startling discovery: the identity type $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)$ behaves like the space of paths from $a$ to $b$, with all the higher-cell coherences of homotopy theory. The univalence axiom then forces 'isomorphism = identity' as a theorem.

Plan. Sections 1–2 are the apparatus of dependent type theory: judgments, contexts, the Π/Σ duo and Curry–Howard. Section 3 introduces identity types and the path-space picture. Sections 4–5 are HoTT proper: univalence and higher inductive types. Section 6 sketches Voevodsky's simplicial-set model — what makes the syntax a coherent foundation.

1. Judgments and contexts

The primitive notion of type theory is the judgment. There are four basic forms:

judgmentread as
$\Gamma\vdash A\;\mathsf{type}$'in context $\Gamma$, the expression $A$ is a type'
$\Gamma\vdash a:A$'in context $\Gamma$, the term $a$ has type $A$'
$\Gamma\vdash A\equiv B\;\mathsf{type}$'$A$ and $B$ are definitionally equal types'
$\Gamma\vdash a\equiv b:A$'$a$ and $b$ are definitionally equal terms of $A$'

A context $\Gamma$ is a finite list $(x_1:A_1,\,x_2:A_2(x_1),\,\ldots,\,x_n:A_n(x_1,\ldots,x_{n-1}))$ where each $A_i$ is a type in the previous context. Variables in a context are distinct, and later types may depend on earlier ones — that's where the 'dependent' in dependent type theory enters. Well-formedness is itself a judgment, $\Gamma\;\mathsf{ctx}$, derivable by an obvious recursion.

Why judgments instead of "$a\in A$". In set theory, $\in$ is a global relation: any term can in principle be tested for membership in any set. In type theory, '$a:A$' is meaningful only as the conclusion of a derivation. You can't ask 'is $\pi:\mathbb{N}$?' as a yes/no question — there is simply no derivation. This makes type-checking decidable in nice cases, and it makes the foundation computational: every well-typed term reduces.

The widget below builds up a context one variable at a time and pretty-prints the typing derivation.

<script>
/* =========================== Context builder =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#ctx-svg'), out=$('#ctx-out'), sel=$('#ctx-scenario');
  const back=$('#ctx-step-back'), fwd=$('#ctx-step-fwd');
  // each scenario: list of context entries, each {var, type, depHTML, deps:[indices of earlier vars]}
  const scenarios = [
    [
      {v:'n', t:'ℕ', deps:[]},
      {v:'v', t:'Vec(ℝ, n)', deps:[0]},
      {v:'f', t:'Vec(ℝ, n) → ℝ', deps:[0]}
    ],
    [
      {v:'m', t:'ℕ', deps:[]},
      {v:'n', t:'ℕ', deps:[]},
      {v:'M', t:'Mat(ℝ, m, n)', deps:[0,1]}
    ],
    [
      {v:'G', t:'Graph', deps:[]},
      {v:'x', t:'Vert(G)', deps:[0]},
      {v:'y', t:'Vert(G)', deps:[0]},
      {v:'p', t:'Path(G, x, y)', deps:[0,1,2]}
    ],
    [
      {v:'A', t:'Type', deps:[]},
      {v:'B', t:'Type', deps:[]},
      {v:'f', t:'A → B', deps:[0,1]}
    ]
  ];
  let scenarioIdx = 0;
  let step = 1; // 1..n entries shown

  function clamp(){
    const n = scenarios[scenarioIdx].length;
    if(step < 1) step = 1;
    if(step > n) step = n;
  }

  function render(){
    clamp();
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const ctx = scenarios[scenarioIdx];
    const W = 620, H = 220;
    const padX = 30, padY = 36;
    const colW = (W - 2*padX) / Math.max(1, ctx.length);
    // colors
    const FILL_ON  = 'rgba(126,224,214,0.14)';
    const FILL_OFF = 'rgba(154,164,178,0.07)';
    const STR_ON   = 'var(--cyan)';
    const STR_OFF  = 'var(--line)';
    // dep arrows first (later columns -> earlier) for visible variables
    for(let i=0;i<ctx.length;i++){
      if(i >= step) continue;
      for(const d of ctx[i].deps){
        if(d >= step) continue;
        const x1 = padX + d*colW + colW*0.5;
        const x2 = padX + i*colW + colW*0.5;
        const y  = padY + 70;
        const mid = (x1+x2)/2;
        const path = SVG('path',{
          d:`M${x2-22} ${y+24} C ${mid} ${y+72}, ${mid} ${y+72}, ${x1+22} ${y+24}`,
          fill:'none', stroke:'var(--violet)', 'stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'
        });
        svg.appendChild(path);
      }
    }
    // boxes
    for(let i=0;i<ctx.length;i++){
      const on = i < step;
      const cx = padX + i*colW + colW*0.5;
      const cy = padY + 50;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:cx-colW*0.45, y:cy-22, width:colW*0.9, height:46, rx:8, ry:8,
        fill: on ? FILL_ON : FILL_OFF, stroke: on ? STR_ON : STR_OFF, 'stroke-width':1.4}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:cx, y:cy+5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill: on ? 'var(--ink)' : 'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `${ctx[i].v} : ${ctx[i].t}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // label
    const lbl = SVG('text',{x:W/2, y:18, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lbl.textContent = 'context Γ — left-to-right; dashed arrows = type depends on this earlier variable';
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
    // judgment
    const judg = SVG('text',{x:W/2, y:H-14,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill:'var(--yellow)','font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace'});
    let pieces = [];
    for(let i=0;i<step;i++) pieces.push(`${ctx[i].v}:${ctx[i].t}`);
    judg.textContent = `Γ = ( ${pieces.join(', ')} )    ⊢  Γ ctx`;
    svg.appendChild(judg);
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const ctx = scenarios[scenarioIdx];
    const lines = [];
    lines.push('derivation of  Γ ctx :');
    lines.push('');
    lines.push('  · ctx              (empty context)');
    let acc = [];
    for(let i=0;i<step;i++){
      const en = ctx[i];
      const depStr = en.deps.length ? `  uses {${en.deps.map(d=>ctx[d].v).join(',')}}` : '';
      const head = acc.length ? `  ( ${acc.map(a=>a.v+':'+a.t).join(', ')} )` : '  ·';
      lines.push(`${head}  ⊢  ${en.t}  type${depStr}`);
      acc.push(en);
      lines.push(`  ⇒ ( ${acc.map(a=>a.v+':'+a.t).join(', ')} ) ctx`);
    }
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`free vars: { ${acc.map(a=>a.v).join(', ')} }`);
    lines.push(`step ${step} / ${ctx.length}`);
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  sel.addEventListener('change', ()=>{ scenarioIdx = parseInt(sel.value,10); step = 1; render(); });
  back.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ step--; render(); });
  fwd.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ step++; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

2. Π and Σ types

The two type-formers that bring dependence to life are Π (dependent function) and Σ (dependent pair).

$\Pi_{x:A} B(x)$$\Sigma_{x:A} B(x)$
introduce$\lambda x.\,b(x)$ where $b(x):B(x)$$(a,b)$ where $a:A,\ b:B(a)$
eliminateapply: $f(a):B(a)$project: $\mathrm{pr}_1(p):A$, $\mathrm{pr}_2(p):B(\mathrm{pr}_1 p)$
compute$(\lambda x.b)(a)\equiv b[a/x]$$\mathrm{pr}_1(a,b)\equiv a$, $\mathrm{pr}_2(a,b)\equiv b$
logic side$\forall x.\,B(x)$$\exists x.\,B(x)$
set-theory sidesection of the bundle $B\to A$total space of the bundle $B\to A$

When $B$ does not actually mention $x$, $\Pi_{x:A} B$ collapses to the function type $A\to B$ and $\Sigma_{x:A} B$ collapses to the product $A\times B$. The dependent versions are how type theory talks about vectors of length $n$, polynomials of degree $\le d$, or matrices of shape $(m,n)$ without escaping into a metatheory.

Curry–Howard slogan. $\forall \leftrightarrow \Pi$, $\exists \leftrightarrow \Sigma$, $\Rightarrow \leftrightarrow \to$, $\land \leftrightarrow \times$, $\lor \leftrightarrow +$, $\neg P \leftrightarrow P\to\bot$. A program of type $T$ is a constructive proof of the proposition $T$.

The widget evaluates a small λ-calculus term step by step. Watch β-reduction unfold.

<script>
/* =========================== λ-reduction stepper =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#lam-svg'), out=$('#lam-out'), pick=$('#lam-pick');
  const stepBtn=$('#lam-step'), resetBtn=$('#lam-reset');
  // strings only — the redexes are picked by left-to-right beta on a parenthesised string
  const programs = [
    ['(λx.λy.x) a b', 6],
    ['(λx.λy.y) a b', 6],
    ['(λf.λx.f (f x)) g a', 8],
    ['(λx.x x) (λx.x x)', 4]
  ];
  let progIdx = 0;
  let history = [];

  function reduce(s){
    // a tiny ad-hoc β-reducer for the four hand-picked terms:
    // we look for "(λX.M) N " and substitute. M, N are atomic or balanced parens.
    // We bail out / mark stuck if no redex.
    const m = s.match(/^\(λ([a-z])\.([\s\S]+)\)\s+([^\s()]+|\([^()]*(?:\([^()]*\)[^()]*)*\))(.*)$/);
    if(!m) return null;
    const [, v, body, arg, rest] = m;
    // capture-free substitution body[arg/v] (our examples avoid clashes)
    const sub = body.replace(new RegExp(`(?<![a-zA-Z])${v}(?![a-zA-Z])`,'g'), arg);
    return (sub + rest).trim();
  }

  // Better: use a hard-coded redex chain per scenario (so we don't need a
  // production parser for divergence detection).
  const chains = [
    ['(λx.λy.x) a b', '(λy.a) b', 'a'],
    ['(λx.λy.y) a b', '(λy.y) b', 'b'],
    ['(λf.λx.f (f x)) g a', '(λx.g (g x)) a', 'g (g a)'],
    ['(λx.x x) (λx.x x)', '(λx.x x) (λx.x x)', '(λx.x x) (λx.x x)']  // self-similar
  ];

  function reset(){
    progIdx = parseInt(pick.value,10);
    history = [chains[progIdx][0]];
    render();
  }

  function step(){
    const chain = chains[progIdx];
    const i = history.length;
    if(i >= chain.length) return;
    history.push(chain[i]);
    render();
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W = 620, H = 200;
    const padX = 30, top = 28;
    const stepH = 28;
    const cur = history[history.length-1];
    // render history as stacked lines (most recent at bottom highlighted)
    const max = Math.min(history.length, 5);
    const startIdx = Math.max(0, history.length - max);
    for(let k=startIdx; k<history.length; k++){
      const y = top + (k-startIdx)*stepH;
      const isLast = k === history.length-1;
      const t = SVG('text',{x:padX, y:y, 'font-size':14,
        fill: isLast ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--mute)',
        'font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace'});
      t.textContent = (k===0 ? '   ' : '→  ') + history[k];
      svg.appendChild(t);
      if(isLast){
        // underline
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padX-4, y1:y+6, x2:padX+history[k].length*8.4 + 30, y2:y+6,
          stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'2 4'}));
      }
    }
    // status badge
    const chain = chains[progIdx];
    const isDiv = (progIdx === 3);
    let status;
    if(isDiv && history.length >= 4){
      status = '↻ diverges (Ω self-replicates)';
    } else if(history.length >= chain.length){
      status = '✓ normal form reached';
    } else {
      status = `step ${history.length-1} / ${chain.length-1}  — click step to reduce`;
    }
    const stTxt = SVG('text',{x:padX, y:H-16,'font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    stTxt.textContent = status;
    svg.appendChild(stTxt);
    renderReadout();
  }

  function renderReadout(){
    const cur = history[history.length-1];
    // free-variable scan: lower-case letters that aren't immediately preceded by λ.
    const free = new Set();
    const bound = new Set();
    cur.replace(/λ([a-z])/g, (_,v)=>{ bound.add(v); return _; });
    for(const m of cur.matchAll(/(?<![a-zA-Z])([a-z])(?![a-zA-Z])/g)){
      if(!bound.has(m[1])) free.add(m[1]);
    }
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`current term : ${cur}`);
    lines.push(`bound vars   : { ${[...bound].join(', ')} }`);
    lines.push(`free vars    : { ${[...free].join(', ')} }`);
    lines.push(`reductions   : ${history.length - 1}`);
    lines.push('');
    if(progIdx === 3){
      lines.push('this is Ω = (λx.xx)(λx.xx). β-reducing gives back Ω: an');
      lines.push('infinite β-chain inside untyped λ-calculus.  Crucially, Ω');
      lines.push('is NOT typable in simply-typed λ-calculus — types rule it out.');
    } else {
      lines.push('β-rule:  (λx.M) N  ⟶  M[N/x]');
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  pick.addEventListener('change', reset);
  stepBtn.addEventListener('click', step);
  resetBtn.addEventListener('click', reset);
  reset();
})();
</script>

3. Identity types

For every type $A$ and every $a,b:A$ we form the identity type $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)$. It has exactly one constructor:

$$\mathsf{refl}_a : \mathsf{Id}_A(a,a)$$

and one elimination rule, the J-rule (path induction): to define a function out of $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)$ for arbitrary $a,b$, it suffices to specify what it does on $\mathsf{refl}_a$ for arbitrary $a$. From J alone one derives:

  • concatenation $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)\to \mathsf{Id}_A(b,c)\to \mathsf{Id}_A(a,c)$, written $p\cdot q$;
  • inversion $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)\to \mathsf{Id}_A(b,a)$, written $p^{-1}$;
  • action of any function $f:A\to B$ on identifications: $\mathsf{ap}_f:\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)\to \mathsf{Id}_B(f(a),f(b))$;
  • transport along $p$: if $P:A\to\mathsf{Type}$ and $u:P(a)$, then $p_*(u):P(b)$.
Path-space picture. Read $A$ as a topological space, $a,b:A$ as points, $p:\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)$ as a path, $\mathsf{refl}_a$ as the constant path. Concatenation, inversion, and $\mathsf{ap}_f$ are then literally path concatenation, path reversal, and post-composition with a continuous map. This is the picture HoTT takes seriously.

The widget below shows two endpoints in a small square; drag one of them to see how the path between $a$ and $b$ moves, and watch its homotopy class read out.

Path space Id_A(a,b)
drag a or b; the path stretches; refl is the loop at a point

<script>
/* =========================== Path-space widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#path-svg'), out=$('#path-out');
  const W=620, H=260;
  let A = {x: 180, y: 130};
  let B = {x: 440, y: 130};
  let dragging = null;

  function clamp(p){
    p.x = Math.max(40, Math.min(W-40, p.x));
    p.y = Math.max(40, Math.min(H-40, p.y));
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // panel
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:20, y:20, width:W-40, height:H-40, rx:10, ry:10,
      fill:'rgba(126,224,214,0.04)', stroke:'var(--line)', 'stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    const lblA = SVG('text',{x:30, y:38,'font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lblA.textContent = 'type A (a "space")';
    svg.appendChild(lblA);

    const dx = B.x - A.x, dy = B.y - A.y;
    const dist = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
    const same = dist < 6;

    // path between A and B
    if(same){
      // refl loop: small circle at A
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:A.x+22, cy:A.y, r:18, fill:'none',
        stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:A.x+22, y:A.y-26,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,
        fill:'var(--yellow)','font-style':'italic'});
      t.textContent = 'refl_a';
      svg.appendChild(t);
    } else {
      // curved path
      const mx = (A.x+B.x)/2;
      const my = (A.y+B.y)/2 - 30;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{
        d:`M${A.x} ${A.y} Q ${mx} ${my} ${B.x} ${B.y}`,
        fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2.4
      }));
      // arrow at end
      const ang = Math.atan2(B.y-my, B.x-mx);
      const ah = SVG('path',{
        d:`M${B.x} ${B.y} L ${B.x - 10*Math.cos(ang-0.4)} ${B.y - 10*Math.sin(ang-0.4)} L ${B.x - 10*Math.cos(ang+0.4)} ${B.y - 10*Math.sin(ang+0.4)} Z`,
        fill:'var(--cyan)'});
      svg.appendChild(ah);
      const t = SVG('text',{x:mx, y:my-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,
        fill:'var(--cyan)','font-style':'italic'});
      t.textContent = 'p : Id_A(a, b)';
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // endpoints
    function ep(p, label, color){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:p.x, cy:p.y, r:8, fill:color, stroke:'#000','stroke-width':1.5,
        style:'cursor:grab','data-pt':label}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:p.x, y:p.y-14, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,
        fill:color,'font-weight':700});
      t.textContent = label;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    ep(A, 'a', 'var(--pink)');
    if(!same) ep(B, 'b', 'var(--violet)');
    else {
      // overlap badge
      const t = SVG('text',{x:A.x, y:A.y-14, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,
        fill:'var(--pink)','font-weight':700});
      t.textContent = 'a = b';
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    renderReadout(same, dist);
  }

  function renderReadout(same, dist){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`endpoints  : a = (${A.x.toFixed(0)}, ${A.y.toFixed(0)}),  b = (${B.x.toFixed(0)}, ${B.y.toFixed(0)})`);
    lines.push(`euclid dist: ${dist.toFixed(1)} px  (visualisation only)`);
    lines.push('');
    if(same){
      lines.push('a = b (definitionally),  so  Id_A(a, b)  is inhabited by  refl_a.');
      lines.push('refl_a  is the canonical "constant path" at a.');
    } else {
      lines.push('a ≠ b (as drawn).  An element  p : Id_A(a, b)  asserts');
      lines.push('an identification of a with b.  Picture: a path from a to b.');
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('  · concat:   p · q : Id_A(a, c)   when q : Id_A(b, c)');
      lines.push('  · inverse:  p⁻¹  : Id_A(b, a)');
      lines.push('  · ap_f(p)  : Id_B(f a, f b)   for f : A → B');
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  function ptFromEvent(e){
    const r = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const sx = (e.clientX - r.left) * (W/r.width);
    const sy = (e.clientY - r.top)  * (H/r.height);
    return {x:sx, y:sy};
  }
  svg.addEventListener('pointerdown', e=>{
    const tag = e.target && e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-pt');
    if(tag === 'a'){ dragging = A; }
    else if(tag === 'b'){ dragging = B; }
    if(dragging){ svg.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId); }
  });
  svg.addEventListener('pointermove', e=>{
    if(!dragging) return;
    const p = ptFromEvent(e);
    dragging.x = p.x; dragging.y = p.y;
    clamp(dragging);
    render();
  });
  svg.addEventListener('pointerup', e=>{
    if(dragging){ svg.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId); }
    dragging = null;
  });

  $('#path-coincide').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ B.x = A.x; B.y = A.y; render(); });
  $('#path-spread').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ A = {x:180, y:130}; B = {x:440, y:130}; render(); });
  $('#path-loop').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ B.x = A.x + 0.5; B.y = A.y + 0.5; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

4. The univalence axiom

For any two types $A,B$ in a universe $\mathcal{U}$, there is a canonical map

$$\mathsf{idtoeqv}: (A=B) \longrightarrow (A\simeq B)$$

built from path induction: an identification $p:A=B$ transports any element of $A$ to one of $B$, and the inverse operation transports back. Voevodsky's univalence axiom says this canonical map is itself an equivalence:

$$ \mathsf{ua}: (A=B) \;\simeq\; (A\simeq B). $$

Consequences are immediate and dramatic:

  • If $A\simeq B$, then there is an identification $A=B$. Isomorphism is identity.
  • Every property invariant under equivalence is automatically invariant under identification — and vice versa. So you can transport a theorem from a structure to anything equivalent.
  • It contradicts Streicher's axiom K (uniqueness of identity proofs): the universe at $(\mathbb{B},\mathbb{B})$ has the swap equivalence as a non-reflexive identification.
  • It implies function extensionality ($\Pi\,x.\,f(x)=g(x)$ implies $f=g$).
Use case. If $A$ is the type of finite groups and $B$ the type of bijections of $A$ with itself, univalence says proving a property of finite groups up to isomorphism really does prove it 'on the nose' — no boilerplate to transport along a chosen isomorphism. The bookkeeping vanishes.

The widget below shows two finite types that happen to be equivalent (a $2$-element type and the booleans), and lets you toggle the canonical equivalence (identity) vs the swap. Both produce identifications $\mathbb{B}=\mathbb{B}$ under univalence — and they're different.

Equivalence ↔ identification
two equivalences B → B give two distinct identifications B = B

<script>
/* =========================== Univalence toggle =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#univ-svg'), out=$('#univ-out');
  const idBtn=$('#univ-id'), swBtn=$('#univ-swap');
  let mode = 'id'; // 'id' | 'swap'

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    idBtn.classList.toggle('active', mode==='id');
    swBtn.classList.toggle('active', mode==='swap');
    const W=620, H=240;
    // two columns: left B, right B
    const Lx = 160, Rx = 460;
    const tt = 70, ff = 160;
    function panel(x, label){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x-90, y:30, width:180, height:H-60, rx:12, ry:12,
        fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:x, y:24, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = label;
      svg.appendChild(t);
      drawNode(svg, x, tt, 'tt', {r:18, stroke:'var(--cyan)'});
      drawNode(svg, x, ff, 'ff', {r:18, stroke:'var(--cyan)'});
    }
    panel(Lx, 'B (left)');
    panel(Rx, 'B (right)');
    const c1 = mode==='id' ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)';
    const c2 = c1;
    if(mode === 'id'){
      drawArrow(svg, [Lx+22, tt], [Rx-22, tt], {color:c1, markerId:'uv-id-1', label:'tt ↦ tt', labelSide:0});
      drawArrow(svg, [Lx+22, ff], [Rx-22, ff], {color:c2, markerId:'uv-id-2', label:'ff ↦ ff', labelSide:0});
    } else {
      drawArrow(svg, [Lx+22, tt], [Rx-22, ff], {color:c1, markerId:'uv-sw-1', curve:30, label:'tt ↦ ff'});
      drawArrow(svg, [Lx+22, ff], [Rx-22, tt], {color:c2, markerId:'uv-sw-2', curve:-30, label:'ff ↦ tt'});
    }
    // path-bar at top
    const py = 6;
    const pcLabel = SVG('text',{x:W/2, y:18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,
      fill: mode==='id' ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)'});
    pcLabel.textContent = mode==='id' ? 'p_id : Id_𝒰(B, B) — the trivial identification' : 'p_swap : Id_𝒰(B, B) — the swap identification';
    svg.appendChild(pcLabel);
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push('equivalence  : e : B ≃ B');
    lines.push(mode==='id' ?
      '             tt ↦ tt,  ff ↦ ff   (identity)' :
      '             tt ↦ ff,  ff ↦ tt   (swap)');
    lines.push('');
    lines.push('univalence  ⇒  ua(e) : B = B');
    lines.push('');
    lines.push('Different e ⇒ different ua(e).  Two distinct elements');
    lines.push('of  Id_𝒰(B, B)  — refuting axiom K (UIP).');
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(mode==='id' ?
      'note: ua(id) is itself the reflexive identification refl_B.' :
      'note: ua(swap) ≠ refl_B  in the universe.');
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  idBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ mode='id'; render(); });
  swBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ mode='swap'; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

5. Higher inductive types

An ordinary inductive type — like $\mathbb{N}$ generated by $0$ and $\mathsf{succ}$ — specifies point constructors. A higher inductive type (HIT) additionally takes path constructors, and recursively higher-cell constructors. The canonical example is the circle:

$$ S^1 \;\;\text{generated by}\;\; \mathsf{base}:S^1, \quad \mathsf{loop}:\mathsf{Id}_{S^1}(\mathsf{base},\mathsf{base}). $$

That is the entire definition. From it one can reconstruct, inside the type theory, the fact that $\pi_1(S^1)\simeq\mathbb{Z}$ — the generator is $\mathsf{loop}$, and iterating it produces every integer winding number. Other examples:

HITgenerators
interval $I$$0, 1:I$, $\mathsf{seg}:\mathsf{Id}(0,1)$
$2$-sphere $S^2$$\mathsf{base}:S^2$, $\mathsf{surf}:\mathsf{Id}_{\Omega(S^2)}(\mathsf{refl},\mathsf{refl})$
suspension $\Sigma A$$\mathsf{N},\mathsf{S}$, $\mathsf{merid}: A\to\mathsf{Id}(\mathsf{N},\mathsf{S})$
pushout $B\sqcup_A C$injections + a path constructor identifying the two copies along $A$
set-quotient $A/{\sim}$$[\cdot]:A\to A/{\sim}$, paths $\mathsf{eq}:a\sim b\to\mathsf{Id}([a],[b])$, and a truncation
Why it works. An ordinary inductive type's elimination rule says 'to map out, define what to do on each constructor'. For HITs the same recipe extends: to map $S^1\to X$, give a point $x:X$ and a loop $\ell:\mathsf{Id}(x,x)$. The recursion principle then computes coherently with the path constructor.

The widget shows $S^1$ as the HIT $(\mathsf{base},\mathsf{loop})$. Click to add additional iterations of the loop; watch the winding number tick.

(function(){
  const svg=$('#cir-svg'), out=$('#cir-out');
  let n = 1;  // signed winding number

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=280;
    const cx = 310, cy = 140, R = 90;
    // base circle
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx, cy, r:R, fill:'none',
      stroke:'var(--line)', 'stroke-width':1.5, 'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    // wound path: |n| full circles, direction by sign
    const turns = Math.abs(n);
    const dir = n >= 0 ? 1 : -1;
    if(turns >= 1){
      // animated-looking spiral via slight radius variation
      const segs = 60 * turns;
      let d = '';
      for(let i=0;i<=segs;i++){
        const t = i/segs;
        const ang = dir * (t * 2 * Math.PI * turns) - Math.PI/2;
        const rr = R + Math.sin(t * 6 * Math.PI) * 1.5;
        const x = cx + rr*Math.cos(ang);
        const y = cy + rr*Math.sin(ang);
        d += (i===0?'M':' L') + x.toFixed(2) + ' ' + y.toFixed(2);
      }
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2.4}));
    }
    // base point
    drawNode(svg, cx, cy - R, 'base', {r:11, stroke:'var(--yellow)', fontSize:11});
    // labels
    const t1 = SVG('text',{x:cx, y:30, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    t1.textContent = 'S¹  generated by  base : S¹  and  loop : Id(base, base)';
    svg.appendChild(t1);
    const t2 = SVG('text',{x:cx, y:H-22, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill:'var(--yellow)','font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace'});
    t2.textContent = `current path: loop^${n}   ∈  π₁(S¹) ≅ ℤ`;
    svg.appendChild(t2);
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`winding number n = ${n}`);
    if(n === 0){
      lines.push('this is refl_base : Id(base, base) — the constant path.');
    } else {
      const sign = n > 0 ? '+' : '−';
      lines.push(`element of  Ω(S¹, base) :   loop^${n}`);
      lines.push(`under the iso  Ω(S¹) ≅ ℤ:   maps to ${n}`);
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(`identification: a path that wraps base around the circle ${Math.abs(n)} times (${sign} direction).`);
    }
    lines.push('');
    lines.push('inside HoTT: π₁(S¹) ≅ ℤ is a *theorem* (Licata–Shulman),');
    lines.push('not an axiom — derived from the HIT definition + univalence.');
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  $('#cir-add').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ n++; render(); });
  $('#cir-sub').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ n--; render(); });
  $('#cir-zero').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ n=0; render(); });
  render();
})();

6. Models of HoTT

Syntax must mean something. A model of HoTT is a categorical structure that interprets every type, term, and judgment, validating all the rules. The flagship model is Voevodsky's:

  • Types ↦ Kan complexes (fibrant simplicial sets, presenting $\infty$-groupoids).
  • Dependent type $B$ over $A$ ↦ a Kan fibration $B\to A$.
  • $\Sigma_{x:A} B(x)$ ↦ total space of the fibration.
  • $\Pi_{x:A} B(x)$ ↦ space of sections (fibred mapping space).
  • Identity type $\mathsf{Id}_A(a,b)$ ↦ path object $A^I$ at the pair $(a,b)$.
  • Universe $\mathcal{U}$ ↦ a univalent universe classifying small Kan fibrations.

Univalence holds because, by construction, the path space of the universe at $(A,B)$ matches the space of equivalences $A\simeq B$. Higher inductive types have models too: $S^1$ in the model is a Kan complex weakly equivalent to the topological circle.

Why a model matters. A consistent model proves the theory consistent. Voevodsky's construction is what tells us HoTT is a coherent foundation, not a syntax that might secretly prove $0=1$. Subsequently other models (cubical, Bezem–Coquand, Orton–Pitts) made constructive sense of univalence — leading to cubical type theory with computational univalence.

The widget below sketches how a single type $A$ unfolds, in the model, into an $\infty$-groupoid: points, paths between points, paths between paths, and so on.
